content.claim: France's conventions ruralité establish reciprocal three-year contracts between State (via rector and prefect) and local elected officials (mayors' associations, departmental councils), in which the State maintains teaching posts and staffing allowances while municipalities commit to reorganizing school networks, with co-financing available via rural territorial equipment grants
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Effectiveness is contested: inspection-general study found conventions only partially succeeded in restructuring school networks, with uneven territorial results
Success was strongly conditional on stakeholder buy-in; most conventions remained focused on short-term organizational issues rather than integrated territorial and pedagogical strategy
No evidence that conventions replicate effectively in centralized systems (like post-2013 Hungary) where municipal governments lack independent budget levers
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
